comments.body:
Hello Ans,

We have identified the specific cause for each order and here is what you need to do to get your labels out.

For order #29505, please allow us some time to check the case further. We will update you soon.
If the order has to be dispatched urgently, you can generate a label for the order outside the app on fedex.com and fulfill the order.

For order #29507, the order contains a custom (non-active) product. The app can generate the FedEx label for this order, if you use the Auto-Generate Label option. Once the label is generated, automatic fulfillment in Shopify will not occur for custom products, so you will need to fulfill the order manually in Shopify using the tracking number from the generated label.

Please try generating the label for #29507, and let us know if tif you face any difficulty.

Thanks and regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
Hello Ans,

The label for order #29606 cannot be generated because the shipping rates are failing, and changing the signature options will not resolve this. The root cause is that Hong Kong does not use postal codes, so the address for Michael Wong at Wanchai has no postal code, which causes FedEx to reject the request entirely.

To fix this, please update the recipient address directly in Shopify Admin before generating the label:

1. Go to Shopify Admin > Orders > #29606
2. Click Edit on the shipping address
3. Enter 000000 in the Postal Code field (this is the standard placeholder FedEx requires for Hong Kong addresses)
4. Save the address change
5. Retry the label using the app.
6. Once rates load, generate the label as normal

This should unblock the label immediately. Please let us know if it goes through after updating the address.

Thanks and regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive

May 11, 2026, 20:35 GMT+5:30 

Hello Ans,
 
The label for order #29606 cannot be generated because the shipping rates are failing, and changing the signature options will not resolve this. The root cause is that Hong Kong does not use postal codes, so the address for Michael Wong at Wanchai has no postal code, which causes FedEx to reject the request entirely.
 
To fix this, please update the recipient address directly in Shopify Admin before generating the label:
 
1. Go to Shopify Admin > Orders > #29606
2. Click Edit on the shipping address
3. Enter 000000 in the Postal Code field (this is the standard placeholder FedEx requires for Hong Kong addresses)
4. Save the address change
5. Retry the label using the app.
6. Once rates load, generate the label as normal
 
This should unblock the label immediately. Please let us know if it goes through after updating the address.
 
Thanks and regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive

The label for order #29606 cannot be generated because the shipping rates are failing, and changing the signature options will not resolve this. The root cause is that Hong Kong does not use postal codes, so the address for Michael Wong at Wanchai has no postal code, which causes FedEx to reject the request entirely.
 
To fix this, please update the recipient address directly in Shopify Admin before generating the label:
 
1. Go to Shopify Admin > Orders > #29606
2. Click Edit on the shipping address
3. Enter 000000 in the Postal Code field (this is the standard placeholder FedEx requires for Hong Kong addresses)
4. Save the address change
5. Retry the label using the app.
6. Once rates load, generate the label as normal
 
This should unblock the label immediately. Please let us know if it goes through after updating the address.
 
Thanks and regards,
Touseef
Team PluginHive
